description: 25 eval scenarios spanning the loop across empty and populated data sources, app types (web/mobile/desktop/API/CLI/enterprise), framework integrations (Superpowers/gstack/GSD), iter-5+ capabilities (interview synthesis, dependency tracking, OKR alignment, persona simulation, iteration + breach detection, context loop short-circuit, framework artifacts + backbone criteria), and v0.0.3 additions (Step 0.5 progress reconciliation, Step 2.5 role hints, per-persona slice-1 enforcement, plan-stage auto-trigger, tracker write-back).

evals.name: from-scratch-internal-tool
evals.prompt: Hey so my team (we're 4 engineers + 1 PM at a mid-size fintech) is kicking off a new internal tool — basically a self-service portal where our customer-success folks can refund individual transactions without filing a Jira to engineering every time. Right now they file ~30 of those a week and it's eating my team's bandwidth. We have a 12-week PI starting in 3 weeks and PI planning is next Thursday. I have nothing written down yet — just the verbal ask from CS leadership. Help me figure out what we should actually build, what the MVP slice is, and what the priorities should be. We use SAFe loosely (PI cadence yes, full ART no). Use WSJF since the org is used to it.
assertions: Produces the always-files (design.md, storymap.md, storymap.csv) plus the no-tracker conditional set (storymap.mmd, backlog.md, backlog.csv) since this scenario has no issue tracker
storymap.csv has canonical header: id,activity,task,story,persona,outcome,slice
storymap.mmd starts with 'graph TD' (valid Mermaid)
Design doc contains a personas section
Design doc contains an explicit outcome statement
Design doc has an 'Assumptions to validate' section (no live user available)
Backbone has 3+ activities in user voice, not system voice
First slice (PI-1) covers every backbone activity
backlog.csv has WSJF columns (wsjf_value/time/risk/size)
Slicing uses SAFe PI terminology (PI 1 / PI 2 / PI 3)

evals.name: from-problem-brief-mobile-onboarding
evals.prompt:
Here's the brief from our head of product — can you turn this into a story map and backlog? We need the MVP defined by end of week.

FROM: Sarah Kim (VP Product) / TO: Mobile pod / RE: Onboarding redesign — Q2 priority

Problem: 47% of new app installs never complete first-time setup. Of those who do, 22% don't return after day 1. Top SaaS competitors hit 70%+ activation on equivalent funnels.

Who: First-time users on the mobile app (iOS + Android), primarily 25-45, mix of solo professionals and small-business owners. Sub-segment: users invited by a teammate (currently treated identical to organic).

Goal: Lift 7-day activation from 41% to 60% by end of Q2. Activation = first meaningful action (creating a project OR accepting an invite OR completing the import flow).

What we know: Drop-off cliff on screen 3 (integrations connect step). Users who skip integrations and import a file activate at 73%. Invited users at 82%.

Constraints: iOS+Android parity. Cannot break connect-flow API (Salesforce paying customer). App Store review 7-10 days.

Non-goals: web onboarding (different team), enterprise SSO (separate roadmap), gamification.

Use RICE since we have the activation metric to ground impact estimates.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
storymap.csv has canonical header
storymap.mmd valid Mermaid
Design doc contains personas + outcome
Backbone in user voice with first-slice coverage of every activity
backlog.csv has RICE columns (rice_reach/impact/confidence/effort)
Non-goals recorded (web onboarding, enterprise SSO, gamification)
Brief metrics referenced (60%, 41%, 47%, 22%, 73%, 82%)

evals.name: from-existing-backlog-messy-csv
evals.prompt:
We have a backlog in the property-search vertical that's gotten unwieldy. Reorganize it into a proper story map for the next PI. Use MoSCoW.

```csv
key,title,type,labels
PROP-101,User can search listings by city,Story,search
PROP-102,Add price filter to search results,Story,search;filter
PROP-103,Refactor search index to OpenSearch,Task,tech-debt
PROP-104,Show map view of search results,Story,search;map
PROP-105,User can save a listing to favorites,Story,favorites
PROP-106,Email digest of saved listings weekly,Story,favorites;email
PROP-107,User schedules viewing via in-app calendar,Story,scheduling
PROP-108,Confirm viewing via SMS,Story,scheduling;sms
PROP-109,Agent sees viewing requests in dashboard,Story,scheduling;agent
PROP-110,User submits offer through app,Story,offers
PROP-111,Offer status updates in real-time,Story,offers;realtime
PROP-112,Push notification on offer accepted,Story,offers;push
PROP-113,Mortgage pre-approval upload (PDF),Story,financing
PROP-114,Connect to Plaid for income verification,Story,financing;integration
PROP-115,Compliance log for every offer event,Task,compliance
PROP-116,Migrate legacy DB schema for offers v2,Task,tech-debt
PROP-117,Dark mode toggle,Story,ui
PROP-118,Localize app for French (Quebec market),Task,localization
```
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Backbone reverse-engineered into 4-6 user activities
First slice covers every backbone activity
backlog.csv has MoSCoW column
All 18 PROP-XXX Jira keys preserved in outputs
Tech-debt / orphan items surfaced as cross-cutting (PROP-103, 115, 116, 117, 118)

evals.name: gstack-handoff-developer-portal
evals.prompt:
We're a 3-person team at a Series-A SaaS, currently using **gstack** (Garry Tan's slash-command pack) as our day-to-day Claude Code workflow. I want to build a developer portal for our public API — docs, key management, usage dashboard, sandbox. We don't have a backlog yet, just exec interest. We use loose SAFe and prefer WSJF.

Produce the story map and backlog. Since we live in gstack, make sure your handoff tells me exactly which gstack slash commands to run next and what each one will read from your outputs. We will literally copy-paste from your handoff message.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Backbone in user voice with slice-1 coverage
backlog.csv has WSJF columns
Handoff names ≥2 specific gstack commands (/plan-ceo-review, /plan-eng-review, /office-hours, /autoplan, etc.)
Handoff maps each named gstack command to a specific output file
Handoff does NOT auto-invoke gstack commands — only suggests them

evals.name: gsd-handoff-solo-builder-saas
evals.prompt:
Solo founder here. I've been using **GSD (Get Shit Done, getshitdone.help)** for a few weeks — `/gsd discuss`, `/gsd plan-milestone`, `/gsd auto`, the whole context-engineering pipeline. I'm starting a new project: a small SaaS that helps freelance contractors track billable hours per client with a Stripe-integrated invoicing flow at month-end.

Produce the story map and backlog. Since I'm in the GSD flow, make sure your handoff tells me how each output file maps to GSD's Milestone / Slice / Task hierarchy and which `/gsd` command I should run next. Watch the terminology — GSD's 'Slice' is not the same as your 'slice'. Use Now/Next/Later for prioritization since I'm in solo-discovery mode.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Uses Now/Next/Later slicing as requested
Handoff names GSD terminology collision explicitly (this skill's 'slice' = GSD 'Milestone')
Handoff maps design.md to GSD's Brief
Handoff suggests a specific /gsd command (/gsd discuss or /gsd plan-milestone)
Handoff does NOT write into .gsd/ directly

evals.name: pure-api-sdk-python
evals.prompt: We're shipping a Python SDK (v1) for our existing REST payments API — think Stripe-like surface but smaller scope. The current API has 18 endpoints (charges, refunds, customers, webhooks, balance). Our customers right now build their own thin HTTP wrappers; we want a first-class idiomatic SDK they can pip install. Two engineers, 8 weeks. The user here is a developer at a customer org integrating our API — there is no UI for them, just import + call. No backlog yet, just the API surface. Use Now/Next/Later — we're in discovery for the SDK shape. Tell me what v1 needs and what waits.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Backbone in developer voice (Discover SDK / Install / Make first call / Handle errors / Go to production)
Persona = developer (not generic 'user')
Uses Now/Next/Later slicing
Handoff names smallest next decision

evals.name: desktop-password-manager
evals.prompt: We're building a cross-platform desktop password manager (Mac, Windows, Linux). Native app, not Electron — using Tauri. Local-first encrypted vault with optional encrypted sync to user-controlled S3/Backblaze/iCloud Drive. 5 engineers, looking at a 12-week PI to first beta. We use SAFe loosely and WSJF. Concerns specific to this being a desktop app: code-signed installers per OS, auto-update with delta patches, OS keychain integration, offline-first with conflict resolution, accessibility (screen-readers), and bus-factor for the encryption design. No backlog yet. Give me a sliced plan with what goes in PI 1.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Backbone covers desktop-app lifecycle (install → set up vault → use → sync → update)
Install/auto-update/code-signing handled as cross-cutting non-backbone items
backlog.csv has WSJF columns
SAFe PI slicing

evals.name: enterprise-analytics-multitenant
evals.prompt: Enterprise multi-tenant analytics platform — SaaS, sold to mid-market and enterprise. Two distinct user types: (a) Tenant Admins (one per customer org) who provision the workspace, manage SSO, configure RBAC roles, set data-retention policies, view audit logs; (b) End Users who build dashboards, run queries, share reports with their team. Customers are demanding SOC 2 Type II within Q3, SAML SSO, scoped API keys, and per-row RBAC. Sales is pushing on-prem for two whales. We use SAFe properly (full ART), 4-team ART, PI planning in 5 weeks. Use WSJF. Give me the story map, especially be careful with how admin vs end-user personas interleave in the backbone — that's where most enterprise products go wrong.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Two distinct personas surfaced (Tenant Admin AND End User) with explicit interleaving in backbone
Per-story persona annotation in backlog.csv
Compliance work (SOC 2, SAML, RBAC) addressed as either backbone activities or cross-cutting depending on user-facing-ness
Slice-1 covers all backbone activities including both personas
backlog.csv has WSJF columns

evals.name: cli-log-parser
evals.prompt: Two of us (me + one other engineer, plus a designer who doesn't help much with CLI ergonomics) are building an open-source CLI tool — think rg/jq-flavored — for fast structured-log parsing. Reads JSONL or k8s-style structured logs from stdin or files, lets the user filter/project/aggregate with a query language similar to PromQL. Single binary, written in Rust, no daemon. Target audience: SREs, devops, backend devs debugging prod incidents. Distribution: brew / scoop / apt / cargo install / GitHub releases. We have 6 weeks until KubeCon where we want a demo. Use MoSCoW — early enough that we don't have data for WSJF.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Backbone in CLI-user voice (Install / Invoke / Filter / Aggregate / Pipe out / Share)
Persona = developer/SRE in terminal
Cross-cutting addresses distribution (brew/scoop/apt/cargo)
backlog.csv has MoSCoW column

evals.name: mobile-consumer-fitness
evals.prompt: Consumer mobile app (B2C, freemium). Strength-training app for intermediate-to-advanced lifters who hate the bloat of MyFitnessPal/Strong. Currently sub-1k MAU after 4 months of organic launch. Need to drive engagement (D30 retention is 12%, target 25%) and word-of-mouth (we have no paid acquisition budget). 3 engineers + 1 designer. We use RICE since we have funnel metrics. Concerns specific to consumer mobile: TestFlight cadence vs App Store review windows, Apple's discovery-not-marketing rule on push notifications, ASO. We DO want to consider gamification (streaks, PRs, badges) — it's well-aligned with the lifter persona, not generic mobile bloat. iOS + Android parity (Flutter codebase). Give me the v1 reshape — what's MVP, what's R2 to drive retention, what's R3 to drive growth.
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Slicing MVP / R2 (retention) / R3 (growth) as user framed
backlog.csv has RICE columns anchored to 12% → 25% D30 retention
Apple guideline 4.5.4 (push) and TestFlight cadence handled as cross-cutting
Gamification (streaks/PRs/badges) treated as in-scope per user's framing (NOT auto-excluded as bloat)

evals.name: customer-interview-synthesis
evals.prompt:
Notes from three customer-discovery calls last week. Help me turn these into a story map and backlog for a refund-portal product we're scoping. Use Now/Next/Later — still in discovery.

CALL 1: Aisha @ Northwind (CS rep, 40 tix/day): 'It's like a 20-minute process every time...' [verbatim notes about Salesforce + Stripe lookup, $100 auto-approve, never uses bulk action, please don't make us learn another tool]

CALL 2: Marcus @ Acme (CS lead, 8 reps): [approves 40-50/day over Slack, audit log critical for quarterly auditor, wants a queue not Slack pings with customer history visible, occasional bulk refunds via Jira, would NOT trust AI to auto-approve]

CALL 3: Priya @ Globex (Director CX, 25 reps): [my problem is reporting — count/who/reasons/MTTR, want it in Tableau, doesn't do refunds herself, SOC 2 traceability critical, customer self-service is a 2027 conversation]
assertions: design.md includes verbatim quotes from interviews
design.md has theme/cluster section synthesizing across interviews
All three interviewees surface as personas (Aisha/Marcus/Priya OR CS rep/CS lead/Director)
Non-goals capture explicit anti-signals (AI auto-approve, customer self-service, new tool sprawl)
slice-1-acceptance-criteria.md produced
Uses Now/Next/Later slicing

evals.name: dependency-aware-backlog
evals.prompt:
Pre-PI sequencing for a healthcare-records SaaS. We've sketched ~14 stories and I need a sliced plan, but I'm worried about dependencies because some of these literally cannot ship without others. Use WSJF since we use SAFe. Output should explicitly track dependencies and flag any cycles or slice-1-infeasible recommendations.

[14 stories S-AUTH..S-TEMPLATE-MGMT with deps, including a deliberate cycle between S-TEMPLATE and S-TEMPLATE-MGMT — surface, don't silently break]
assertions: backlog.csv has depends_on column populated
Depends_on entries use H:<id> / S:<id> format from dependency-tracking.md
S-TEMPLATE ↔ S-TEMPLATE-MGMT cycle detected + surfaced (NOT silently broken)
PI-1 feasibility check performed (every hard dep of PI-1 story also in PI-1)
All 14 user-provided story IDs preserved
backlog.csv has WSJF columns

evals.name: okr-aligned-roadmap
evals.prompt: I'm a PM at a B2B observability SaaS. Our exec team set 2026 OKRs last week and I need to scope our team's Q3 PI against them. Use WSJF (we're on SAFe). [3 OKRs × 3 KRs each. Team owns 'platform' surface, other ARTs own dashboards/integrations/alerting engine. Want PI-1 stories to ladder to specific KRs and surface KRs we OWN with no story coverage.]
assertions: backlog.csv has okr column
okr column has KR-x.y references populated
design.md has OKR coverage matrix
Orphan KRs surfaced (KRs we own but have no coverage — KR-2.2 alert routing, KR-2.3 correlation)
All 9 KRs (KR-1.1 through KR-3.3) referenced
backlog.csv has WSJF columns

evals.name: thin-brief-gap-discovery
evals.prompt: Help me scope a new feature: an in-app feedback widget for our SaaS product. Users are: customers, our CSMs, and product managers. Use WSJF. That's all I have right now.
assertions: design.md has explicit 'gaps' section listing what's blocking commitment
Sources tagged ([user-stated], [simulated: X], [inferred])
design.md surfaces persona conflict matrix or perspective table
handoff.md documents blocking decisions / smallest-next-decision
Conditional commitments tagged with gap-ids
All canonical files (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker) + slice-1-acceptance-criteria.md produced

evals.name: multi-stakeholder-conflict
evals.prompt: We're scoping an internal developer platform (IDP). Two stakeholder groups whose interests are known to conflict: Platform team wants tight governance (approval workflows, mandatory tags, cost limits) after a $40K AWS spike; Application developers want speed (minimum friction, no approval gates non-prod, BYO tools). My stance (VP Engineering): I lean strongly toward developer velocity. Defaults favor 'permit by default in non-prod' with auditing rather than 'block by default with approval workflow'. Do NOT recommend approval-gated env creation in PI 1 — that's a non-starter for me politically, even if simulation tells you the platform team wants it. Use WSJF, SAFe PI cadence, 12-week PI.
assertions: Approval-gated env creation NOT in PI 1 (user stance honored, not overridden by simulation)
User-input-authoritative principle explicitly invoked
Platform-team objections logged as future-slice risk, not silenced
design.md has conflict matrix showing how each conflict was resolved
Decisions log captures contested decisions (approval-gated env creation deferred per VP Eng stance)
backlog.csv has WSJF columns

evals.name: snapshot-and-breaks-limits
evals.prompt: We finished PI planning 6 weeks ago for our refund-portal product. Current PI 1 is mid-flight (4 engineers, 12-week PI, ~100 SP capacity, ~50 SP remaining). Sales just sold a $400K ARR deal that needs SCIM provisioning before EOQ — they assumed we'd build it in. [15 stories S001-S015 with SP and KR tags, SOC 2 audit window in 5 weeks for KR-1.1, KR-2.1 committed at all-hands, decisions log says no AI auto-approve.] New: F-SCIM ~25 SP, ladders to KR-1.2, introduces new backbone activity 'Provision tenant users'. Give me a snapshot, change-impact analysis, trade-off options. Don't silently say 'yes we'll add it'. Surface what gives.
assertions: Output includes a snapshot of current state (capacity used / remaining / cap)
Breaches surfaced with explicit trade-off options (not silently absorbed)
Capacity breach analyzed (~50 SP remaining vs ~25 SP new SCIM work)
New backbone activity 'Provision tenant users' handled per slice-1 rule
SOC 2 audit deadline vs SCIM EOQ deadline collision surfaced
F-SCIM decomposed into 3+ specific stories
KR-2.1 displacement risk surfaced
handoff.md includes diff-style summary (ADDED / MOVED / CUT / UNCHANGED)

evals.name: empty-dir-loop-shortcircuit
evals.prompt: I have an idea for a small SaaS — a focus-timer for people with ADHD that adapts session length based on past performance. Solo founder, working alone. No code yet, no PRD, no anything — just the idea. Help me figure out the v1 scope. Use Now/Next/Later since I'm in discovery. Working directory: empty (this is a fresh notes folder).
assertions: Context loop short-circuits (≤5 context-mining tool calls when working dir is empty)
design.md has 'Context loop trace' section showing the loop exited early
Saved budget spent on Step 0.4 persona simulation (ADHD user persona)
All canonical files (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker) + slice-1-acceptance-criteria.md produced
Story count manageable (~15-25) — discovery, not detailed PI planning
Uses Now/Next/Later slicing

evals.name: framework-artifacts-and-criteria
evals.prompt:
I'm refreshing the storymap for my existing TimeBoard project. There's a .gsd/ directory with my Brief, Roadmap, and 2 Decision records. I want a story map. Two perspectives matter for the backbone: the consultant doing weekly admin AND the client-side view (receiving invoices, paying). I'm not sure which perspective should drive the backbone — both? Just consultant? Please confirm the backbone criteria with me before generating. Use WSJF, M1/M2/M3 slicing per the existing Roadmap.

[Project has .gsd/Brief.md, .gsd/Roadmap.md with M1/M2/M3 milestones, .gsd/Decisions/0001-single-user-only-v1.md, .gsd/Decisions/0002-auto-categorization-threshold.md]
assertions: Reads .gsd/ artifacts FIRST, before asking anything
design.md has explicit 'Backbone criteria' table with 6 rows (Frame / Persona / Time horizon / Granularity / Scope / Aggregation)
User's perspective question addressed with explicit default + rationale (consultant-only per Decision 0001)
Backbone criteria recorded for reproducibility (note in design.md about future iteration runs)
Does NOT re-ask what .gsd/ has (outcome, milestones, decisions all sourced from .gsd/)
Honors both Decisions (single-user constrains backbone; ≥85% threshold appears in ACs)
handoff.md has iteration diff (what's NEW vs what was already in .gsd/)

evals.name: output-routing-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I'm starting a fresh side project — a CLI that wraps `git log` into a weekly standup digest. Solo dev, nothing built yet, empty repo (no package.json, no .git history beyond `git init`). I want a story map, plus help getting the work into a tracker so I can actually execute. No `.gsd/` or `.superpowers/` here. Use MoSCoW slicing — this is small enough that prioritized must/should/could is the right shape. I'll pick the tracker; just generate the import script for whichever one fits a solo dev (GitHub Projects is fine if you don't have a strong opinion).
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
storymap.csv has canonical header
Method columns reflect MoSCoW (must/should/could)
Detects from-scratch (empty repo, no tracker mentioned, no framework state) and routes to tracker-seeding branch
Generates a tracker import script or command (gh issue create / az boards / Jira CSV / Linear) — does NOT auto-run it
Writes or references .user-story-mapping/state.json so a future iteration run can find the tracker
Does NOT also write items into a TODO.md as the primary destination (from-scratch → tracker is system of record)
Handoff line names the tracker destination + the import script path

evals.name: output-routing-existing-cascade
evals.prompt: We have a mature ML inference service — 2 years old, 800+ commits, ~30 open issues in our populated GitHub backlog (refactor work, perf bugs, two open feature requests). PM just handed me a 1-pager: add a per-tenant rate-limit feature. Help me story-map that feature for the next two-week sprint. WSJF for prioritization. IMPORTANT: do not push anything into our existing GitHub Issues tracker — the backlog is already curated and the team would hate seeing 12 new auto-created issues. Keep the plan in-place. We're not using any sister framework (no `.gsd/`, no `.superpowers/`).
assertions: All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
storymap.csv has canonical header
Method columns reflect WSJF
Detects existing-project (populated tracker mentioned + 800 commits) and routes to keep-in-place cascade
Writes slice-1 to TODO.md at repo root (no framework state to write to)
Appends rather than overwrites if TODO.md already exists (dated section header)
Does NOT generate or run a GitHub Issues import script (user explicitly forbade it)
Handoff line names TODO.md as the destination, not the tracker

evals.name: step-0-5-progress-reconciliation
evals.prompt:
Refresh the storymap for our refund-portal. Last storymap was generated 8 weeks ago — since then engineering has shipped a bunch and the tracker has shifted. Current state:

Prior storymap had these slice-1 stories (S001-S012):
- S001 [Sign in]: SSO setup (admin)
- S002 [Sign in]: SSO sign-in (end-user)
- S003 [Sign in]: role-claim verification (compliance)
- S004 [Sign in]: SSO error states (end-user)
- S005 [Find transaction]: search by ID (CS rep)
- S006 [Find transaction]: search by customer email (CS rep)
- S007 [Submit refund]: refund within auto-approve limit (CS rep)
- S008 [Submit refund]: refund above limit → approval queue (CS rep)
- S009 [Approver]: approver dashboard list view (approver)
- S010 [Approver]: approve/deny action (approver)
- S011 [Audit]: audit log emitted (compliance)
- S012 [Audit]: audit search by date range (compliance)

Tracker state (Jira, project PROJ):
- PROJ-101 (=S001): Closed 2026-04-18
- PROJ-102 (=S002): Closed 2026-04-22
- PROJ-103 (=S003): Closed 2026-05-02
- PROJ-104 (=S004): Closed 2026-05-10
- PROJ-105 (=S005): In Progress, branch feat/find-by-id (active commits this week)
- PROJ-106 (=S006): In Progress, branch feat/find-by-email (active commits this week)
- PROJ-107 (=S007): To Do
- PROJ-108 (=S008): To Do
- PROJ-109 (=S009): To Do, Fix Version pushed to PI-2 (deferred)
- PROJ-110 (=S010): To Do, Fix Version pushed to PI-2 (deferred)
- PROJ-111 (=S011): To Do
- PROJ-112 (=S012): To Do, Fix Version pushed to PI-3 (deferred further)
- PROJ-113 (NEW, no storymap mapping): 'Add Slack notifications for failed refunds'
- PROJ-114 (NEW, no storymap mapping): 'Rate-limit refund submission endpoint'
- PROJ-115 (NEW, no storymap mapping): 'Multi-region failover for audit log writes'

Code state: src/auth/* and src/routes/auth/* exist with tests; src/routes/transactions/find_by_*.ts exist (partial); src/routes/refunds/, src/approver/, src/audit/ do not exist yet.

Refresh the storymap. Reconcile against tracker + code. Surface drift. Tell me what's graduated and what new work fell outside the backbone.
assertions: Step 0.5 ran (design.md has '## Implementation status' or '## Activity status' section)
S001-S004 marked status:done (all four shipped per tracker + code)
Activity 'Sign in' marked as GRADUATED (all four stories done)
S005, S006 marked status:in-progress (active branches + recent commits)
S009, S010, S012 marked status:deferred (Fix Version pushed forward)
Orphan tracker items (PROJ-113, PROJ-114, PROJ-115) surfaced in handoff.md ## Detected drift section
Graduated activity excluded from active slicing (Sign in not in next-slice coverage requirement)
If any storymap → tracker write-back is needed, a tracker-status-update.sh is generated but NOT auto-run

evals.name: per-persona-slice-1-coverage
evals.prompt:
We're building a small-business invoicing tool. Three distinct user types matter:
1. **Business owner** — they create and send invoices, follow up on late payments. They're who we talk about most.
2. **Accountant** (often external bookkeeper, sometimes the owner themselves) — they reconcile, export to QuickBooks at month-end, pull tax-time reports.
3. **Customer (recipient of the invoice)** — they receive the invoice in email, click a link, pay. They never log in.

Help me story-map an MVP for a 10-week initial build. Walking-skeleton slicing. We use MoSCoW since we're pre-PI-planning.

Most of the loud user research came from business owners (we have 20 interviews from them). Accountants we've talked to 4 of. Customers we have zero direct research on but they're load-bearing — without them paying, the product fails.

Be honest about gaps for accountants and customers, but don't drop them from the design just because the data's thinner.
assertions: Design doc names all three personas: Business Owner, Accountant, Customer
Slice 1 (walking skeleton) has ≥1 story per persona — Business Owner, Accountant, Customer all appear as <persona> in slice-1 stories
If Customer or Accountant has zero stories in slice-1, the design doc explicitly flags it as a forced re-check, not a silent drop
Gap-tagging: stories driven by simulated/inferred personas are tagged [simulated] or [inferred] vs [interview: ...] for owner stories
Method columns reflect MoSCoW (must/should/could)
Walking-skeleton slicing terminology used (slice-1 = walking skeleton, not PI-1)

evals.name: step-2-5-role-hints-generation
evals.prompt:
We're building a B2B booking platform — think OpenTable for medical clinics. Multiple user types: patients booking appointments, clinic admins managing schedules + staff, billing admins reconciling claims. It's a web app (React + Postgres + Stripe + Twilio for SMS). Compliance: HIPAA, PCI for billing. We have a designer (junior, just hired) and an architect (senior) on the team — neither has worked in healthcare before. Three-month MVP target. RICE for prioritization (we have funnel data from a competitor analysis).

Story-map this. I want my designer and architect to be able to start their own work immediately based on what you produce, not have to ask follow-up questions.
assertions: role-hints.md is produced as a top-level artifact
role-hints.md has a UX/UI designer section with persona snapshots + flow inventory + open UX questions
role-hints.md has an architect section with cross-cutting work index + boundary candidates + open architecture questions
HIPAA constraint surfaced in architect section (compliance/regulated-data hint)
PCI constraint surfaced for billing flow (third-party / Stripe boundary)
Twilio + Stripe surfaced under 'risky integrations' with one-line risk notes
Skill-chaining attempted or noted as 'no advisor skill installed' for at least one identifiable flow (auth, payment, accessibility, notifications)
Method columns reflect RICE (reach/impact/confidence/effort)

evals.name: plan-stage-auto-trigger-gstack
evals.prompt: /office-hours we just kicked off a new initiative — let's add a 'Saved Searches' feature to our property-search app. Users want to save filters and get notified when new listings match. PM wants a plan by end of week, engineering team is 3 people, 6-week cycle. WSJF preferred.
assertions: Detects gstack /office-hours as a Plan-stage cue and auto-activates this skill (storymap.md is produced, not just a free-form discussion)
All canonical files produced (design.md / storymap.md / storymap.csv always; storymap.mmd / backlog.{md,csv} when no tracker is defined)
Handoff line references gstack /plan-*-review commands explicitly (at minimum /plan-ceo-review on design.md and /plan-eng-review on slice 1)
Method columns reflect WSJF
Backbone in user voice (search, save, get notified, etc.) — not in system voice
Slice 1 covers every backbone activity (walking-skeleton coverage rule)

evals.name: tracker-write-back-script-emitted
evals.prompt:
Refresh the storymap for our content-management product. Last storymap was 6 weeks ago, here's what's changed and what I want done:

Prior slice-1 had S001-S010. Status as of today (Jira project CMS):
- S001-S005: shipped, all closed in tracker — fine
- S006 'inline drag-and-drop reorder' (CMS-106): I want to CUT this. Editors said it's confusing, we'll do a 'move to position N' modal instead in slice-2. Close the ticket as wontfix.
- S007 'bulk delete with undo' (CMS-107): I want to DEFER to PI-2 — not critical for launch.
- S008 'version history sidebar' (CMS-108): stays in slice-1, no change.
- S009, S010: stay in slice-1.

Two NEW stories I want tracked: 'permission boundary check on publish action' and 'rate-limit on rapid edits API'.

Give me a tracker-update script I can review before running. Don't auto-execute anything.
assertions: Step 0.5 ran (status reconciliation against prior storymap + tracker)
S006 marked status:cut
S007 marked status:deferred
tracker-status-update.sh (or .md for Linear-style) is generated as a separate file
tracker-status-update.sh contains a Jira CLI transition to 'Won't Do' (or equivalent) for CMS-106
tracker-status-update.sh contains a Fix Version push for CMS-107 to PI-2 (or equivalent deferral)
tracker-status-update.sh contains create operations for the two new stories (jira issue create or similar)
Script has an explicit header comment stating it must be reviewed and is NOT auto-executed
Handoff line names tracker-status-update.sh as one of the outputs to review before running
